description: Hoenn fire-starter evolution-line mirror at adjacent collector numbers (DAA-022 ↔ DAA-023). Torchic (Fire-only chick, no. 255) evolves at level 16 into Combusken (Fire/Fighting, no. 256) and then into Blaziken (Fire/Fighting, no. 257) — the canonical Gen-III Hoenn fire starter line from Pokemon Ruby and Sapphire (Game Freak, 2002). The two adjacent prints carry the line's signature type-shift-on-first-evolution as a posture-and-energy gradient: Torchic's peaceful chick-of-warmth register (small chick on rocky ground, ambient warm glow, single Live Coal attack) yields to Combusken's action martial-kick register (kick-pose mid-action, swirling flames, Smash Kick + Heat Beak Burn-status). The Hoenn-fire-starter line is one of three Gen-III starter lines and shares the type-shift-on-first-evolution pattern with its Treecko and Mudkip line-mates.
citations: [Pokemon Ruby and Sapphire (Game Freak, 2002) primary canon for the starter line]
other_candidates_considered: Fletchling DAA-151 ↔ Fletchinder DAA-031 — Kalos Fletchling line evolution mirror; deferred because Fletchling already has a substantive Related-cards section explicitly listing Fletchinder, so the Connections-section mirror would be partial duplication of existing structure. Could be drawn as a follow-up enlightened pass once Fletchinder is trivia'd.

Mareep DAA-055 ↔ CRE-047 — cross-print same-species mirror; deferred because both ends are vision-only (neither trivia'd yet), so the mirror lacks the canonical citation hook that the Hoenn-line mirror gets from Bulbapedia/Pokedex per-species pages. Re-surface after either Mareep print gets a trivia pass.
Pansage DAA-006 ↔ BUS-12 cross-print — already implicitly covered by E1 cohort completion since both prints attach to elemental-monkey-trio; a separate card↔card mirror would be redundant.
Mitsuhiro Arita artist Mr. Nodeley — deferred to a future invocation (one Nodeley node per pass; E3 shipped alolan-regional-forms).
Ghost cluster — premature without focused enrichment of ghost-type cards first.
notes: Two card-endpoints. Both equal nodes. Must be traversable from either side. Hence the mirror. The sigh is professional, not personal. Adjacent collector numbers (DAA-022 next to DAA-023) is the cleanest tell that Game Freak ordered the set's fire-starter line as an in-set diptych — when the set itself encodes the evolution as a pair on adjacent slots, the mirror is the right shape.
